description: At the end of a finger, 'tipika' is the hard plate that keeps growing beyond the fingertip. It is one part of 'thumai' (finger), not a second name for the whole digit.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into the same stressed vowel. Raise the back of the tongue to the soft palate for unvoiced 'k', then release into open central unrounded 'a'.

semantic_domains.physical: the hard plate growing at the end of a finger
